summary: Space requirements for connections in drivers
description:
When connections are created in an application (to connect to mongos), what is the data requirement (we've been told it's 1M)?  Is it the same for each driver or are some more expensive than others?

Thanks,
Lorne

summary: Resync retryable writes test
description:
There are 2 changes to the retryable writes tests:
 # Changed the format of the "failPoint" field, see [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/035c987b0b99a22b49703c64f91195010fa1f6b4#diff-c74e7320652f60d5defb81f16066278eL143]
 # Added \{{retryableErrors.yml}} to test all the retryable error codes added in SPEC-1051 using the failCommand failpoint added in MongoDB 3.7.

summary: Resync CRUD tests to add bulkWrite tests and "ordered" option for bulkWrite and insertMany
Add support for a "comment" parameter to the aggregate command
Common policy for copyright dates in headers and documentation
description:
Per legal, header files should use the following format for date ranges: "<year the file was created>-present". For example:

{noformat}
# Copyright 2009-present MongoDB, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# ...
{noformat}

Replace 2009 with whatever year the file was created.

For documentation footers, the following text should be used:

© MongoDB, Inc 2008-present. MongoDB, Mongo, and the leaf logo are registered trademarks of MongoDB, Inc.

Replace 2008 with the inception year of the project.

Original description follows
-------------------------------------

Different driver projects have different conventions for updating copyright dates in files:

* Never update the year (list year of first creation)
* Update the year in a file whenever the file is modified
* Update all the files to the current year every year

Should we have a common approach?  Does it matter?

As a criteria: if it doesn't really matter, we should choose the approach that requires the least amount of work for engineers.

summary: Initial aggregate command for a change stream should not be retried
description: If any driver has implemented retry on the initial aggregate command, they will need to ensure that aggregate commands are not retried.

summary: Resync retryable writes tests
description: Resync retryable writes spec tests. You probably want to do this before pulling in the changes for DRIVERS-480 so you're only dealing with one or two spec changes and possible test runner changes at a time.

summary: Re-sync transaction spec tests for new UnknownTransactionCommitResult label tests
description: Re-sync transaction spec tests for new UnknownTransactionCommitResult label tests. See changes:  [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/f76bc24087d914dd5f1257b16b817457a7fb4cfa]

summary: Unpin member when read preference changes
description:
The Read Preferences spec siad that a thread's first read in a request pins it to a replica-set member, and stays pinned until the request ends or the thread reads with a preference that the pinned member cannot satisfy. THE CHANGE: When a driver pins a thread to a member, it remembers the read preference used for this read -- the mode, tags, and secondaryAcceptableLatencyMS. If the next read has a different mode, tags, or secondaryAcceptableLatencyMS, unpin the member and start the member-selection process from scratch.

NOTE: All primary operations within the request must use the same socket, even if interleaved with operations on secondaries. (For simplicity, drivers may also use a single socket on each secondary, but this is not required.)

summary: Deprecate cursor snapshot option
description:
The snapshot option was [deprecated in MongoDB 3.6.1|https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/SERVER-32173] and [removed in MongoDB 3.7.4|https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/SERVER-32174]. All drivers should mark this option as deprecated in documentation and remove examples if any. Drivers may raise a warning if it is required if your language. Any tests using the snapshot find option should be skipped for MongoDB >= 3.7.0.

Resync your Command Monitoring tests from the specs repo:

https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/ecbbf828deb82763bbe36343a7bf04d78d0454b5

summary: Call "ping" on a socket that has been idle for socketCheckIntervalMS
description:
Change to specification for single-threaded clients and the meaning of socketCheckIntervalMS.

Before, single-threaded drivers were required to call "ismaster" on a socket that's been idle for socketCheckIntervalMS before using the socket. It was not clear what the driver should do with the ismaster response, or what it should do if ismaster failed.

Instead, what the driver should do is re-check an idle socket right after server selection. If a server is selected that has an existing connection that's been idle for 5 seconds, the driver should attempt to call "ping", not ismaster, since we're not trying to update the topology. If "ping" succeeds, use the socket. If it failed, set the server's type to Unknown and re-enter server selection once, re-discovering the topology if needed (as SDAM already specifies), then proceed from there.

summary: Update ChangeStream spec to detail usage of startAtOperationTime
description:
If drivers have already implemented DRIVERS-464, they need to update their implementation of how they initially store {{startAtOperationTime}}. Instead of setting a default {{startAtOperationTime}} when the ChangeStream is created, drivers must omit a default {{startAtOperationTime}}. When resuming, drivers must use the {{operationTime}} of the initial aggregate command as the default {{startAtOperationTime}} value.

See https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/745debcf4624ec820eb9b138cd8f28fa7b1e5782 for the exact implementation details.

summary: withTransaction() should retry commits after a wtimeout
description:
SPEC-1208 removed logic that would prohibit {{withTransaction()}} from retrying commits after a wtimeout error. Additionally, there are modified spec tests.

Drivers should sync their {{transactions-convenient-api}} spec tests to https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/2f4e7bc914adbc2e1ea2e837b5afceb731aeada8.

summary: rescanSRVFrequencyMS should be rescanSRVIntervalMS
Provide Transactions example for Docs
List Julia driver on Community Supported Drivers webpage
description:
I would like to suggest to add Julia MongoDB driver on https://docs.mongodb.com/ecosystem/drivers/community-supported-drivers/ .

The driver repo is hosted at https://github.com/felipenoris/Mongoc.jl  .

summary: Deprecate parallelCollectionScan helpers
description: The parallelCollectionScan command is removed from the server in 4.1. All drivers MUST deprecate existing parallelCollectionScan helpers and stop testing the helper with MongoDB 4.1+.

summary: Username and password must be URI-escaped
description:
All drivers need to update their Connection String Spec tests and ensure conformance with a slightly modified description of how special characters in username and password are escaped in the URI.

Not just "%", "@", and ":" must be escaped, all delimiters from RFC 3986 must be percent-encoded. Therefore, since "/" must be escaped in username and password, we can split the URI at the first "/" instead of the last. Delimiters are also prohibited in the auth database name, but allow ".".

summary: FLE Test Fixes
description:
Issues with the "Spec Test Format" section:

* client_side_enencryption_opts in the "Spec Test Format" section should be auto_encrypt_opts Fixed in https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/623.
* auto_encrypt_opts should be autoEncryptOpts
* kms_providers should be kmsProviders
* schema_map should be schemaMap
* bypassAutoEncryption is missing from the list of auto encryption options
* Some operations don't have an object field. It should be documented that this defaults to "collection" if omitted.

Issues with the "Use as integration tests" section:

* The json_schema field should be mentioned to describe how the collection should be created.
* The description for setting up the test collection and inserting the test data uses the MongoClient created with the test's clientOptions. For tests with invalid encryption settings (e.g. "an insert with encrypted field on _id errors" in badQueries.json), using this client will cause the data insertion to fail. The test data should be inserted using a different client with no encryption enabled.

Issues with various tests:

* "operation fails with maxWireVersion < 8" in maxWireVersion.json - the error is expected to contain "Auto-encryption requires a minimum MongoDB version of 4.2"  but the spec only says "Drivers MUST raise an error when attempting to auto encrypt a command if the maxWireVersion is less than 8" and does not specify the message.
* "getMore with encryption" in getMore.json - results field should be result.
* Corpus test step 9 - ClientEncryption has no schemaMap option.

summary: Resync transactions tests to include test with session1 in APM expectations

Add test to prove that driver uses "session1" correctly in operations and APM expectations. Drivers should sync tests to [bdf89c3|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/bdf89c370847993bbfad2128345e4498b2707701].

This ticket may pull in changes from previous spec test changes in DRIVERS-550.

Single collection change streams will see the new notification followed by an invalidate entry. Whole db/cluster change streams will only see the collection drop/rename.

See spec change [here|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/381/files]. Drivers should sync tests to [267e260|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/381/commits/267e260d1f4dfd98f5c8d2a1077bc761c3b7f3cd].

description: Now that MongoDB 4.2 has branched from master we need to start testing it in Evergreen. This depends on https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools/issues/65.

summary: Transaction test runner should use "local" read concern when asserting the final collection state
description:
{panel:title=Useful Info|borderStyle=dashed|borderColor=#ccc|titleBGColor=#239eb0|bgColor=#c2d2c2}

*Lead*:
*POCs*:

*Summary*: 
The transaction spec test runner says:
{code}#. For each element in ``outcome``:

   - If ``name`` is "collection", verify that the test collection contains
     exactly the documents in the ``data`` array. Ensure this find uses
     Primary read preference even when the MongoClient is configured with
     another read preference.
{code}
However, when a test configures the client with readConcern=majority and performs a write with w=1, then the subsequent find will run with readConcern=majority and possibly miss the w=1 write depending on the current majority commit point.

To ensure the find remains causally consistent with previous operations we must use "local" read concern while reading the latest data on the primary.

*Final Specification*:
https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/0b8c2e9cef44848a89ec9b5aceeb3f5382879d46

{panel}

summary: Investigate changes in SERVER-31368: Log time spent waiting for other shards in merge cursors aggregation stage
Make 'reIndex' a standalone-only command
Support $out to a different database
Implement $accumulator
Add single responder OCSP certificates to drivers-evergreen-tools
description:
The certificates in drivers-evergreen-tools currently have two OCSP endpoints defined, with the mock responder listening on the port defined in endpoint #2 in order to test whether a driver's TLS library will utilize all endpoints defined in a certificate to determine its revocation status.

 [~john.stewart] and I determined that Java hard-fails when it is unable to reach out to an OCSP endpoint, even if there is another valid endpoint available. Thus in order for Java (and drivers whose TLS library exhibits similar behavior), we must create a series of certificates with only a single OCSP endpoint defined.

summary: Remove connection pool wait queue event class and properties for limiting wait queue size
description: This has been implemented by JAVA-3461, but needs to be done on other drivers that implement wait queues.

summary: Redaction of AWS session token
description:
Looking at the Python driver implementation of AWS auth, it appears to redact the AWS session token in error messages. I do not see this redaction mentioned in https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/auth/auth.rst or https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/auth/tests/mongodb-aws.rst.

If the redaction is desired, should it be described in a spec?

summary: OCSP spec tests reference tlsDisableOCSPEndpointCheck URI option twice
description:
In https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/ocsp-support/tests/README.rst there is the following sentence:

> Specifically, the TLS Options Test has been updated with additional tests for the new URI options tlsDisableOCSPEndpointCheck and ``tlsDisableOCSPEndpointCheck.

Seems like the same option is mentioned twice. If there is only one option I suggest changing the language to reflect this.

summary: Resync SDAM tests to only examine non-unknown server descriptions to determine incompatibility

SDAM spec was updated to require that only non-unknown servers' descriptions are considered when determining topology incompatibility, as well as spec tests were added. Drivers should sync tests to [98e67a|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/a98e67a49ce68a75b2e0b7225f2e1c6ee638d809].

This change may include previous spec test changes in DRIVERS-479 if language ticket associated with that ticket is still open. 

Update: Small change to the new tests committed in  [7b9940|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/7b994052cda126e1352218b7977499fc85f5614b]

summary: Add test that reads ignore db/collection readConcern in transaction
description: See SPEC-1161 for details.

summary: addSpecial in read preference specs
description: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/driver-read-preferences.rst#read-preference-configuration and https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/server-selection/server-selection.rst#read-preference-configuration refer to "addSpecial" which isn't defined anywhere that I can see and is not used anywhere else in the specifications repo. Is this a legacy thing and should it be removed?

summary: Improve ecs role imdsv2 checks
Clarify reasoning behind how SDAM determines if a topologyVersion is stale
description:
When implementing DRIVERS-1187, I was confused as to why one algorithm for determining staleness used {{>=}} and another used {{>}}.
{code:python}def isStaleError(topologyDescription, response):
    currentServer = topologyDescription.servers[server.address]
    currentGeneration = currentServer.pool.generation
    generation = get connection generation from response
    if generation < currentGeneration:
        # Stale generation number.
        return True
    currentTopologyVersion = currentServer.topologyVersion
    # True if the current error's topologyVersion is greater than the server's
    return compareTopologyVersion(currentTopologyVersion, response.get("topologyVersion")) >= 0

# ....

def isStaleServerDescription(topologyDescription, server):
    # True if the new ServerDescription's topologyVersion is greater than
    # or equal to the current server's.
    currentServer = topologyDescription.servers[server.address]
    currentTopologyVersion = currentServer.topologyVersion
    return compareTopologyVersion(currentTopologyVersion, server.topologyVersion) > 0
{code}
[~kevin.albertson] clarified for me that {{isStaleError}} uses {{>=}} because "[a]ny state change would imply a new topologyVersion."

[~matt.broadstone] suggested that such a clarification would be helpful in the pseudocode in the spec and [~jeff.yemin] requested that this ticket be created to track this.

summary: Enable and configure TCP Keepalive by default
description:
h5. Problem Description

keepalive in the Java driver (and other drivers) is disabled by default. This leaves the possibility of leaving downed server connections in the middle of a socket read stuck in a waiting state.

We had a situation where a mongos server crashed leaving 100 open connections on the client side. When we recovered the mongos the Java driver still had 100 bad connections taken from the pool and would not open new ones.

As part of this change, drivers should include in their documentation a link to the [MongoDB Diagnostics FAQ keepalive section|https://docs.mongodb.com/v3.2/faq/diagnostics/#does-tcp-keepalive-time-affect-mongodb-deployments]

h5. Specification

# A driver MUST enable TCP keepalive by default.  This matches the behavior of the MongoDB server.
# A driver MUST deprecate TCP keepalive-related options in the connection string (and any other way that it is configured), as there is no demonstrated benefit to allowing it to be disabled.  This also matches the behavior of the server.
# A driver SHOULD set tcp_keepalive_time to 300 seconds unless it determines that the system default is already less than that. If the driver is unable to determine the system default at all it should not attempt to change it.  This matches the behavior of the server as well.
# A driver SHOULD set tcp_keepalive_intvl to 10 seconds unless it determines that the system default is already less than that.   If the driver is unable to determine the system default at all it should not attempt to change it.  This is not the current behavior of the server, but if accepted here it will be recommended.  The reasoning is that with the default of 75 seconds for this value and a default of 9 probes, the actual time to failure is 300 + (75 * 9) = 975 sec = 16.25 minutes.  With a 10 second interval between probes it becomes a more reasonable 6.5 minutes.
# A driver SHOULD set tcp_keepalive_cnt to 9 probes unless it determines that the system default is already less than that. If the driver is unable to determine the system default at all it should not attempt to change it.
# A driver MUST document how keepalive-related options are configured.  Drivers that can set tcp_keepalive_time and tcp_keepalive_intvl to the values mandated above MUST document that they do so.  Drivers that can not MUST document that they do not and link to appropriate [MongoDB Diagnostics FAQ keepalive section|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/faq/diagnostics/#does-tcp-keepalive-time-affect-mongodb-deployments] for instructions on setting these values at the system level.

summary: Raise error when hint option is provided on unacknowledged writes against any server version
description: A previous version of the CRUD spec said that the driver should only raise errors when the hint option is provided to an unacknowledged write against a server that does not support the hint option. However, the CRUD spec also says that the driver should raise errors whenever the arrayFilters or collation options are provided on any unacknowledged write. The spec has been changed to state that the driver should raise an error when the hint option is provided on unacknowledged writes against any server version.
